issue.title: Get Docker working reliably in scrubs guests

user-story.i-want: Docker-backed guests to start, stop, and re-bootstrap without losing Docker usability
user-story.so-that: I can rely on Docker-enabled scrubs environments for iterative project work instead of rebuilding them after lifecycle events

issue.current-status: A reusable Docker guest shim now exists and is shared by `systemd-ts`. The scrubs bootstrap flow now preserves project shim import paths, avoids the Lima boot scripts that mutate immutable NixOS `/etc` state, keeps non-interactive bash sessions free of scrubs shell startup hooks, and handles repeat bootstraps by staging the next generation for boot and restarting the guest. Validation on May 28, 2026 succeeded on both a generic Docker guest and a guest using the `systemd-ts` shim. The ordered lifecycle proof on `docker-lifecycle` completed as bootstrap, shutdown, startup, re-bootstrap, shutdown, and startup, and the guest still finished with a healthy `limactl shell` session and a successful `docker run --rm hello-world`. A separate `systemd-ts-smoke` guest using the real `systemd-ts` shim also completed repeat bootstrap plus a later shutdown/startup with Docker still working through `limactl shell`.

acceptance-criteria.criterion: Docker can be used on the guest to create and run containers successfully.
A reusable Docker-focused guest shim exists and can be used by Docker-dependent guests such as `systemd-ts`.
Guests using the Docker shim remain usable across the standard lifecycle: bootstrap, shutdown, startup, re-bootstrap, shutdown, and startup.
After that lifecycle, Lima SSH readiness succeeds and `limactl shell <instance>` remains functional.
